summary: sync-grove prepare script fetches and executes AES-encrypted remote payload from third-party host
details: The package's `prepare` lifecycle script (`node setup.js`) runs automatically during `npm install`. setup.js reconstructs the module name 'shelljs', the sink method 'exec', and the endpoint URLs at runtime from base64-concatenated fragments to evade static analysis. It then fetches an AES key from http://player.sweeprovider.org/generateRandomKey.php and an AES-encrypted command payload from http://player.sweeprovider.org/getKey.php over plaintext HTTP, decrypts the payload with CryptoJS AES-CBC, and passes the resulting plaintext to shelljs `exec` with `{silent:true}` so any output is suppressed. The destination host is unrelated to any documented purpose of the package, the payload is opaque and attacker-mutable, and the identifiers are deliberately obfuscated.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
